content.text: Patterns are licensed corporate IP. Rounds are inventoried. Unlicensed weaving is the crime.

A pattern is the thing you weave: a licensed design that banks into rounds and fires like a weapon (the procedure — see Weave & Fire). Patterns are weapons in the data model — tier, Fire cost, damage dice, tags and all. Tags like Pierce and Impact work as printed in the damage rules (see Attacks); conditions like Rattled and Seared work as printed on Conditions.
A full Weave banks a pattern's listed rounds — typically 3. Tier sets the Charge a Weave generates (see Weave & Fire) and prices the license (below).

rows: 1d6 + Rattled to an Adjacent cluster in Medium.
2d6 + Seared, Adjacent cluster of a point in Medium. No roll.
The zone is difficult terrain; the first band move out of it costs +1 AP.
Personal Absorb 2 until your next turn.
3d8; punch-through counts double against Armor HP. The siege pattern.
Move 2 bands ignoring triggers and terrain.

content.text: Mend Weave is the catalog's only healing, and it pollutes the patient: the heal is knit with rift matter, and the bill lands as Charge — then Saturation (see Charge & Saturation).

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The catalog is missing numbers the procedure depends on: per-pattern Weave DCs, per-pattern round counts (only "typically 3" is canon), crit beats for the targeted patterns, and Ember Wreath's Seared rating. None are defined in the rebuild docs.
You don't find patterns; you buy them. Learning one takes a license — credits, priced by tier and provider — plus practice: downtime and LP (how learning works — see Skills).
Wellspring licenses Mend Weave and audits its use. Nobody licenses what the Bloom teaches.

This pattern is licensed, not sold. Possession of woven rounds does not constitute ownership of the design. Audit teams may verify conduit registration at any time. Wellspring thanks you for healing responsibly.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: License pricing (by tier and provider) and the practice requirement (downtime periods, LP totals) are named but never costed in the rebuild docs.

A bootleg is the cracked copy: half the license price, and it shows. Bootlegs Weave at DC +1, and their misfire exposure opens at Charge 6+ instead of the licensed threshold (thresholds — see Charge & Saturation; the Misfire Table — see Weave & Fire). When the street says bootlegs jam, that early window is what it means.
Pressing your own: bootleg production is a crafting question, not a casting one. The Material Cost Law owns it, jam risk included — see Material Cost.

content.label: PARKED
content.text: The catalog beyond the launch ten is parked: flagged for a future pass, not designed. These ten are the whole arsenal.
